evidence: Apologise resonates strongly with relational ontology (core of relationships), catalytic interconnection (triggers forgiveness), essential resonance (touches relationship essence), inclusive symmetry (absorbing blame to protect), and ethical value (moral imperative). It operates at individual scale and involves lived experience, with weaker ties to predictable/contingent contexts and comprehension of wrongdoing.

Apologize resonates most with relational and ethical aspects of defense (e.g., repairing harm through relational bonds, moral responsibility). It catalyzes change (interconnection.catalytic) and touches essential/resonance.essential layers of trust. Scores are low elsewhere as apologies are individual/community-scale acts with no cosmic or structural implications.

Apology resonates most in relational and individual scales (8,5), ethical value (8), and catalyzing change (transformative symmetry, 5). Its semantic meaning (7) and structural patterns (4) tie to defense by addressing harm through relational repair. Lower scores reflect its lack of cosmic/ecosystem scope and non-physical/material nature.

The verb 'body guard' strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical protection), scale.individual (one-on-one safeguarding), and value_generation.functional (practical utility). Moderate scores reflect its relational nature (protector-protected bond) and reliance on predictable routines (lawfulness.predictable).

Bodyguard strongly resonates with scale.individual (10) as it directly protects a single person. Functional value (10) reflects practical protection. Relational ontology (7) highlights the protector-protected bond. Ethical value (3) ties to moral duty in defense. Resonance.essential (3) captures core purpose of protection.

A champion's essence lies in relational bonds (6) and ethical duty (7), catalyzing transformative change (5). Their role is individual-focused (8) and resonates deeply with the core purpose of defense (7).

Championing (as in defending) strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (5) due to its focus on meaning and value in protection. It also aligns with interconnection.resonant (4) as it involves deep mutual support, and symmetry.transformative (4) because it catalyzes positive change. Ethical value (5) underscores its moral imperative.

Charm as a noun in the SOC.defend context strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical objects like amulets) and value_generation.functional (practical protection). It also aligns with symmetry.generative (creating protective effects), scale.individual (commonly used for personal protection), and epistemological.identification (naming the object as a 'charm').

Charm as a defense mechanism operates through interpersonal relationships (ontological.relational), triggers behavioral shifts in others (interconnection.catalytic), and functions as a surface-level tactic (resonance.superficial). It has practical utility (value.functional) and aesthetic appeal (value.aesthetic), with effectiveness depending on context (lawfulness.contingent).

Coinsurance resonates most with shared relational structures (ontological.relational), predictable cost-sharing (lawfulness.predictable), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It operates at individual and structural scales, with essential resonance in its core concept of shared responsibility.

The word 'cover' as a noun in the SOC.defend context strongly resonates with material aspects (physical shields), functional utility (practical protection), and the essential core of defense. It also relates to independence (existing on its own), predictability (expected protection), and comprehension of protective mechanisms.

Cover strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical shielding), scale.individual/community/society (protection at various levels), and value.functional (practical utility). Lower scores reflect contexts like ongoing protection (temporal.continuation), relational dynamics (mutual defense), and symmetry.inclusive (absorbing into protection).

The phrase 'cover for' strongly resonates with ontological.relational (10) as it inherently involves a relationship between protectors and those being shielded. It aligns with value_generation.ethical (8) due to its moral implications of protecting others. Essential resonance (7) reflects its core role in safeguarding from harm. Scale.individual (8) captures its typical interpersonal use.

Defence strongly resonates with physical protection (ontological.material), societal structures (scale.society), and core ethical duty (value_generation.ethical). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential) reflects protection as a foundational human need. Functional utility (value_generation.functional) is clear in practical applications like security systems.

Defend strongly resonates with material protection (walls, shields), individual/community/society scales, and ethical value. The essential resonance captures its core purpose of safeguarding. Symmetry with destructive (as opposed to attack) and predictable lawful patterns (e.g., defense mechanisms) also align. Lower scores reflect its lack of cosmic scale or existential resonance.

The highest scores reflect the defender's essential role (resonance.essential), its practical function (value_generation.functional), and its relational nature (ontological.relational). It also emphasizes continuity (temporal.continuation) and the inclusive dynamic of absorbing threats (symmetry.inclusive).

The highest scores reflect practical utility (functional), moral dimensions (ethical), and core essence of protection (resonance.essential). Defense operates at individual/community/society scales, requires structural/relational organization, and depends on conditions (contingent).

The highest scores reflect core aspects of defense: semantic (core meaning of protection), functional (practical utility), material (physical defenses), and experience (embodied knowing like feeling defensive). Moderate scores align with societal scales, relational dynamics, and ethical dimensions. Lower scores emphasize defense's non-transcendent nature and lack of cosmic scale.

Excusatory resonates strongly with individual defense mechanisms (scale.individual=5), relational dynamics (ontological.relational=3), and ethical implications (value.ethical=3). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential=3) stems from being core to justifying protection, while superficial excuses (resonance.superficial=3) reflect surface-level justifications. Scores above 2 are tied to direct connections to defense via excuses.

Excuse resonates most with semantic layers (ontological.semantic) as it carries meaning in justifying actions. It operates at individual scale (scale.individual), influences through relational dynamics (interconnection.catalytic), and ties to ethical value (value_generation.ethical). Its superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) reflects its role as a surface-level justification in defense.

The word 'excuse' as a defense mechanism strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (5) because it conveys meaning and justification. It operates at an individual scale (3) and relies on contingent contexts (3). Its superficial protective role (3) and relational/transformative dynamics (2-3) align with the defensive intent, while ethical undertones (2) reflect moral ambiguity in justification.

Extenuation resonates most with relational interactions (defending involves relationships between entities), transformative dynamics (changing the nature of threats), functional utility (practical protection), ethical considerations (moral duty to protect), and essential aspects of defense (core to the act of protecting).

Fence strongly resonates with material (physical barrier), functional (utility in protection), and perception (tangible object). Moderate scores reflect individual/community scale, establishment/continuation over time, and superficial resonance as a surface-level defense. The transformative symmetry score (1) acknowledges minimal environmental impact, while ethical/aesthetic/sacred values are absent in this protective context.

Fence as a verb in the context of defense strongly resonates with material (physical barriers), functional value (practical protection), and essential resonance (core of protection). It operates at individual/community/society scales and involves relational dynamics (defending relationships). Predictable lawfulness and contingent threats also align strongly.

Fence in strongly resonates with physical materiality (tangible structure), functional utility (practical protection), and essential resonance (core of defense). It also aligns with structural patterns (boundary design), clear identification (named entity), and predictable nature (expected protective measure).

The phrase 'fend for' strongly resonates with individual-scale action (scale.individual=8) and material/physical protection (ontological.material=5). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential=7) reflects core self-defense, while functional value (value_generation.functional=8) underscores practical utility. The lived experience (epistemological.experience=7) and contingent nature (lawfulness.contingent=5) align with real-world scenarios of independent survival.

Gloss (as a superficial explanation or facade) resonates most with semantic meaning (5), superficial resonance (5), and symmetry.generative (4) when used to deflect harm by creating a protective narrative. Scores reflect its role in obscuring or reframing reality to defend against criticism, tied to information layers (4) and relational dynamics (3).

Guard resonates most with temporal.continuation (ongoing protection), scale.individual/community (physical entities guarding others), ontological.material/relational (physical presence and relationships), and value.functional (practical utility). Scores drop where the concept exceeds human/social scales or involves abstract ethics/symbolism.

Guard strongly resonates with individual protection (scale.individual), practical utility (functional), physical actions (material), and inclusive relational dynamics (symmetry.inclusive). Moderate connections exist to community/society scales, relational aspects, and continuity over time (temporal.continuation).

Hold resonates strongly with temporal.continuation (ongoing protection), ontological.material (physical act of defense), and resonance.essential (core to the act of defending). It also scores moderately in scale.individual/community (scope of protection), epistemological.experience (embodied action), and value_generation.functional/ethical (practical and moral aspects of defense).

Home guard strongly resonates with community-scale defense (scale.community) and material/relational layers (ontological.material, ontological.relational). Its essential role in protection (resonance.essential) and functional/ethical value (value_generation.functional, value_generation.ethical) align with SOC.defend. Scores drop where the concept doesn't inherently imply broader scales or existential resonance.

Immunise strongly resonates with material (physical vaccines), essential (core to defense), predictable (expected outcomes), transformative (enabling immunity), and functional (practical protection). Moderate scores reflect its relational role in host-pathogen dynamics, contingent effectiveness, and ethical implications in public health.

Immunize strongly resonates with individual protection (scale.individual=10) and functional utility (value_generation.functional=10). It aligns with ontological.semantic (5) as it embodies the abstract concept of defense. Temporal.influence (5) reflects its ongoing protective effect, while symmetry.generative (5) highlights how it creates immunity. Scores above 0 are grounded in direct connections to defense mechanisms, biological systems, and societal health practices.

Insurance strongly resonates with functional value (10) as its core purpose is practical protection. It aligns with SOC.defend's semantic meaning (5), operates relationally (4) between insurer/insured, and is societal-scale (4). Predictability (4) and ethical considerations (2) reflect its foundational role in risk mitigation.

The word 'insure' strongly resonates with relational dynamics (ontological.relational:7), functional utility (value_generation.functional:8), and essential protection (resonance.essential:6). It operates predictably (lawfulness.predictable:7) and at individual/community scales (scale.individual:7, scale.community:3). Lower scores reflect its focus on practical, non-existential protection without destructive or cosmic elements.

The word 'justifiable' strongly resonates with ethical value (8), semantic meaning (7), and relational layers (5), as it pertains to morally or legally grounded defense. Scores reflect its ties to societal/legal frameworks (scale.society:5), understanding (6), and transformative dynamics (3). Lower scores emphasize its focus on justification within specific contexts rather than broad existential or temporal dimensions.

Justification strongly resonates with ethical value (4) as it often involves moral reasoning, semantic meaning (3) as it constructs rationale, and generative symmetry (2) as it creates defensive arguments.

The word 'justificative' strongly resonates with the semantic layer (meaning of defense) and touches the essential core of why protection is needed. Ethical considerations (right/wrong reasons for defense) and the structural/logical underpinning of justification also score highly. Lower scores reflect its abstract, non-temporal, and scale-agnostic nature.

Justificatory resonates most with semantic meaning (5), ethical value (4), and structural reasoning (3). It underpins the justification required to defend against harm, aligning with moral arguments (ethical), logical frameworks (structural), and the meaning behind protective actions (semantic).

The highest scores reflect that 'justifier' operates at the semantic level (meaning/value of defense), generates defensive actions (symmetry.generative), touches the core essence of protection (resonance.essential), and provides functional/ethical value. Lower scores align with its role in relational structures and scale-dependent contexts.

Justify resonates most with semantic slots tied to meaning (ontological.semantic), moral value (value_generation.ethical), and relational structures (ontological.relational). It operates at individual/community scales and involves creating structured arguments (ontological.structural/informational). Lower scores reflect its lack of direct temporal or cosmic scope, and its avoidance of destructive symmetry.

The highest scores reflect 'keep' emphasizing ongoing action (continuation), physical protection (material), practical utility (functional), and moral duty (ethical). Lower scores align with relational dynamics (e.g., protecting relationships) and the contingent nature of defense depending on threats.

The highest scores reflect 'look out' as a vigilant, individual action rooted in sensory perception (epistemological.perception:5) and practical utility (value_generation.functional:5). It resonates superficially with danger awareness (resonance.superficial:3) and operates predictably (lawfulness.predictable:3) while connecting catalytically to defensive actions (interconnection.catalytic:2).

Mitigation strongly resonates with functional value (practical harm reduction), temporal continuity (ongoing efforts), and structural/semantic layers (material and meaning-based defenses). It operates at societal/community scales and aligns with transformative symmetry (changing risk dynamics) and essential resonance (core protective intent).

Mitigative resonates most strongly with temporal influence (ongoing reduction of harm), scale (individual/community/society/ecosystem mitigation efforts), ontological material/structural (physical and organizational measures), and value generation (practical utility and ethical duty). Scores drop to 0 for non-relevant slots like cosmic scale or existential resonance.

Mitigatory resonates most strongly with reducing harm's material impact (ontological.material 4), functional utility (value.functional 5), and its essential role in core protection (resonance.essential 4). It operates across individual/community/society scales (3-2 scores) and relies on relational dynamics (ontological.relational 3). Scores are minimized for non-physical or existential aspects, adhering to SOC.defend's protective scope.

Mothproofing strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical application), value_generation.functional (practical protection), and symmetry.inclusive (absorbing threats). Scores for temporal transformation (material change) and lawfulness.predictable (reliable defense) also reflect its defensive nature. Lower scores reflect its individual-scale focus and lack of resonance with abstract or large-scale concepts.

Overprotect strongly resonates with relational dynamics (ontological.relational=8) as it defines relationships between protector and protected. It carries ethical weight (value_generation.ethical=7) due to potential harm, touches the core of those relationships (resonance.essential=7), and often manifests destructively (symmetry.destructive=6) when excessive. It operates primarily at an individual scale (scale.individual=8) and through lived experience (epistemological.experience=5).

Overprotection resonates strongly with individual relationships (ontological.relational), ethical dilemmas (value_generation.ethical), and its destructive excess (symmetry.destructive). It touches the core of autonomy vs protection (resonance.essential) and is experienced through lived interactions (epistemological.experience). Its persistence (temporal.continuation) and predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable) further anchor its resonance.

Palisade strongly resonates with material, structural, and semantic layers (physical barrier, organizational patterns, and meaning of defense). It aligns with community-scale protection, predictable defense strategies, and functional utility. Scores drop where its role is abstract (e.g., ethical, existential) or unrelated to its physical, defensive essence.

Palisade as a verb strongly resonates with material and structural layers (physical barriers), community-scale protection, and functional value. Scores peak in defense-related aspects like permanence, establishment, and essential resonance with core protective intent. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to abstract or large-scale concepts.

Palliation resonates most with material (physical actions to mitigate harm), semantic (intent to protect), functional (practical utility), and relational (connections in care) slots. Scores reflect its role in ongoing, individual-level protection without transformative change.

Preservation strongly resonates with **scale.ecosystem** (8) and **ontological.semantic** (8) due to its role in safeguarding interconnected systems and meaning. It aligns with **lawfulness.predictable** (8) as it relies on consistent efforts, and **value_generation.functional** (8) for its practical utility. High scores in **temporal.continuation** (7) and **temporal.permanence** (7) reflect its focus on enduring protection. **experience.comprehension** (7) and **value_generation.ethical** (7) highlight its link to understanding and moral duty.

Preservation strongly resonates with maintaining structural integrity (ontological.structural), ethical duty (value.ethical), and ongoing efforts (temporal.continuation). It operates at ecosystem scales (scale.ecosystem) and involves experiential comprehension (experience.comprehension). The symmetry.inclusive score reflects absorbing threats, while existential resonance ties to core survival.

Preservation strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (maintaining over time), scale.ecosystem (protecting interconnected systems), ontological.material (physical preservation), and value_generation.functional/ethical (practical and moral imperatives). Scores reflect its core purpose of sustaining existence without fundamental change.

The word 'preserver' strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (8) as protection requires ongoing effort. It scores highly in value.functional (8) and value.ethical (7) due to practical and moral dimensions of protection. Ontological.semantic (6) reflects its core meaning of safeguarding value. Resonance.essential (7) captures its fundamental role in preserving existence.

Protect strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical shielding), scale.individual/community/society (common scopes of protection), resonance.essential (core purpose of defense), value_generation.functional (practical utility), and symmetry.inclusive (absorbing into safety). Protection strongly resonates with material and relational layers (physical shields, familial bonds), societal scale (national defense), ethical value (duty to safeguard), and essential resonance (core purpose of safety). Scores dip where connections are indirect or speculative.

The word 'protective' strongly resonates with **scale.individual** (direct personal protection), **ontological.material** (physical safeguards), **value_generation.functional** (practical utility), and **resonance.essential** (core purpose of defense). The word 'protector' strongly resonates with material and relational aspects (physical presence and relationships), functional utility (practical protection), and ethical values (duty to defend). Rationalisation resonates most with defense through its role in justifying protective actions (ontological.semantic, value_generation.functional). Rationalization (as defense) strongly resonates with semantic layers (meaning/justification), ontological structural/informational aspects (organizational patterns and data), and generative symmetry (creating justifications). Safeguard strongly resonates with semantic (core meaning of protection), structural (organizational systems), and functional (practical utility) slots. It operates across material, informational, relational, and structural layers (ontological), and aligns with absorbing threats (symmetry.inclusive) through predictable measures (lawfulness.predictable).

The word 'screen' resonates most with physical barriers (ontological.material), protection at individual/community levels (scale.individual/community), and the structural role of defense (resonance.structural). Screening as defense involves detecting threats (temporal.indication, epistemological.perception) at scales from individual to societal (scale.individual/society). Shield resonates strongly with material existence (physical protection), functional utility (practical defense), and the inclusive dynamic of absorbing harm. It also aligns with relational dynamics between protector and protected, and its essential role in defense touches core ontological meaning.

Shielding strongly resonates with protection's core essence (resonance.essential=8) and practical utility (value.functional=10). Shielder strongly resonates with **scale.individual/community/society** (protection at various levels), **ontological.material/relational/structural** (physical shields and organizational defense), **interconnection.resonant/synchronous** (coordinated protection efforts), and **resonance.essential** (core purpose of protection). Shielding strongly resonates with material (physical protection), functional value (practical utility), and essential resonance (core essence of protection). It operates at individual/community/society scales, persists over time (continuation), and aligns with predictable patterns. Ethical value arises from its moral purpose, while relational ontology reflects protector-protected dynamics.

The phrase 'stand up' in a defensive context strongly resonates with **value_generation.ethical** (9) and **ontological.semantic** (8), as it embodies moral duty and meaning. The phrase 'stick up' strongly resonates with individual-scale defense (scale.individual), material and relational layers (ontological.material/relational), and ethical/functional value (value_generation.ethical/functional). Stockade strongly resonates with defense through its physical structure (material, structural), communal protection (community scale), and functional utility. Support in the context of defense strongly resonates with functional utility (practical protection), relational dynamics (alliances or mutual aid), and the essential core of defense itself. Support resonates most strongly with protection's material and relational aspects (physical shielding, protector-protected bonds). Scores are low elsewhere due to focus on direct protection rather than abstract scales or existential themes.

The word 'supporter' strongly resonates with **scale.individual** (10) as it describes a single entity's role in protection. **ontological.relational** (7) reflects its role in fostering relationships through defense. **value_generation.functional** (7) and **value_generation.ethical** (4) highlight practical and moral dimensions of support. **symmetry.generative** (3) and **symmetry.transformative** (2) align with creating safety and altering situations. **resonance.essential** (4) underscores its core purpose of protection.

Supportive strongly resonates with relational (core essence of connection), ethical (moral dimension of protection), and ontological.semantic (meaning of safeguarding). It also aligns with temporal.continuation (ongoing protection), interconnection.resonant (deep mutual support), and symmetry.inclusive (absorbing others into safety).

The highest scores reflect 'surround' as a physical barrier (material layer), core to defense (essential resonance), and a common tactic (predictable). It operates at individual/community scales, involves perception/identification, and creates functional value through protection.

Surround resonates most with physical/material barriers (ontological.material), relational dynamics between protectors and threats (ontological.relational), and inclusive protection (symmetry.inclusive). It operates at individual/community/society scales, relies on sensory perception and embodied experience, and aligns with predictable defensive strategies. Territorial defense strongly resonates with establishment and continuation (ongoing protection of stable boundaries), societal scale (nations/regions), material/structural aspects (physical land and borders), independent action (self-defense), essential resonance (core to territorial existence), predictable patterns (common to defend territories), functional utility (practical protection), ethical value (moral duty to protect), and epistemological perception/identification (marking and naming territories).

Territorial reserves are physical (material) areas established (temporal.establishment) to protect ecosystems (scale.ecosystem) and societal interests (scale.society). Their ethical (value.ethical) and functional (value.functional) purposes align with defense. The essential resonance (resonance.essential) reflects their core role in shielding against harm.

Wall resonates strongly with material (physical barrier), structural (defensive system), and essential (core of protection). Wall as a verb in defense strongly resonates with material existence (physical barrier), structural patterns (defensive architecture), and essential protection (core of defense). It operates at individual/community scales, influences over time, and provides functional utility.

The word 'ward' strongly resonates with material existence (physical spaces like hospital wards), functional utility (practical protection), and essential resonance (core purpose of defense). Scores reflect its tangible presence, relational role in protection, and predictable establishment as a defensive structure.

Ward as a verb in the SOC.defend context strongly resonates with material protection (ontological.material), the essential nature of defense (resonance.essential), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It moderately connects to individual-scale actions (scale.individual), ongoing persistence (temporal.continuation), and catalyzing responses (interconnection.catalytic). The highest scores reflect the warder's physical presence (ontological.material 5), core role in defense (resonance.essential 4), and systemic integration (resonance.structural 3).
